chapters.title: 1.0 Who We Are
chapters.bodyHtml:
We aren't big …

Our company values, objectives, and philosophy are a direct reflection of the kind of consultant company we want to inspire: where everyone involved is driven to deliver world-class services with meaningful, positive impact.

Simply put: we strive to be the best. And we're looking for more like us. Our recruiting radar is always on seeking the next superstar from every discipline.

Our Vision

Road towards empowering total safety culture by becoming the best fully integrated organization in Occupational Safety and Health

chapters.title: 2.0 Onboarding
chapters.bodyHtml:
Your First Day

Your first day will be fairly slow.

First, you'll sign the paperwork and read this handbook!

Then, you'll have a chance to meet everyone including your mentor. They will show you around and then walk you through your first task/project.

Your First Week

Make mistakes. Ask questions. We all did it.

We also encourage you to talk with everyone! Grab people to go get coffee, share your perspective on where you think you can provide the most impact to the company, and as we said earlier: ASK QUESTIONS!

Your First Month

We are extremely proud.

Within a month at AMIOSH, you will be working directly with clients to help solve their problems.

By the end of your first month, you'll be ready to dive in head first. You will have learned a lot from us and we will have learned a lot from you.

chapters.title: 4.0 Company Policies
chapters.bodyHtml:
4.1 Team Member Classification
CategoryYears’ of Service
A1–2 years of service
B2–5 years of service
C5 years and above
4.1.1 Permanent Team Member

Permanent team members are employees who are normally scheduled to work five (5) days per week. Permanent team members are eligible for all of the benefits set forth in the following pages according to the category.

4.1.2 Probation Team Member

Probation team members are employees who are normally scheduled to work five (5) days per week. Probation team members are not eligible for most benefits. Probation team members should consult the management team to determine those benefits, if any, for which they are eligible.

4.1.3 Contract Team Member

Contract team members are employees who are deployed to the client’s site in which their working days/hours are according to site requirements. Contract team members are eligible for benefits that are stipulated in their contract.

4.1.4 Temporary Employee

Temporary team members are employees who are employed to work on special projects for short periods of time, or on a "fill-in" basis. These positions are not intended to be a part of continuing operations. The employment status of temporary team members will not be changed as a result of an extension of employment in excess of that originally planned. Temporary employees are not eligible for benefits.

4.1.5 Intern

Interns are students who are undergoing an internship/practical program. AMIOSH provides placement for the program. The responsibilities of an intern lie according to terms and conditions between the university and AMIOSH.

4.2 Probation Period

The first six (6) months of your employment shall be a probationary period. During this probationary period your performance and suitability for continued employment will be monitored.

4.3 Government’s Employment Scheme

The probationary period according to the host of the scheme. AMIOSH shall comply with the terms and conditions set out in the contract of employment.

4.4 Attendance Logs

AMIOSH’s office team members are required to thumb print at the designated location while contract team members' attendance shall be verified by project person-in-charge using digital attendance. Monitoring and recording absence/no-show to the office/work days for HR’s records to sync with the Annual Leave of all staff and interns.

What’s Considered Absence/No Show:

  1. No official leave application made before/after the absence
  2. No official information on whereabouts known to HR either in Whatsapp group or Operation Calendar
  3. No records of Medical Cert provided upon return to work
  4. Failure to reason your absence with approved justifications

Note:

  1. The absence/no show to office or work may affect your salary/ allowance in terms of deductions
  2. If you have informed your absence to any person, it is your responsibility to ensure that person communicates the information to HR
  3. If you have been granted 'Special Arrangement' leave, please DM HR the proof for verification
4.5 Termination of Employment

a. In cases of voluntary resignation, the employee must submit an official written resignation letter to the management team. We are practicing one-month notice. We also consider you to resign if you don’t come to work for five consecutive days without notice.

b. In cases of involuntary dismissal,

  1. For cause termination is justified when an employee breaches their contract, engages in illegal activities (e.g. embezzlement), disrupts our workplace (e.g. harasses colleagues), performs below acceptable standards or causes damage or financial loss to our company. The following action steps will be taken:
    1. Consultation face-to-face with management
    2. Show-cause letter (First warning if the same issue arises)
    3. Termination letter within one-month notice
  2. Without cause termination refers to redundancies or layoffs that may be necessary if we cease some of our operations or re-assign job duties within teams. We will follow applicable laws regarding notice and payouts.
4.6 Work-From-Home (WFH) Policy

WFH is allowed within reason and justification upon approval from management. Please apply WFH to the management the day before. The following reasons are applicable for WFH:

  • Natural disaster (Inability to commute to workplace)
  • Needs of quarantine of dependent family members
  • Government’s instruction
4.7 OHS Policy

Aligned with ISO 45001:2018 certification for AMIOSH, we are practicing Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) policy which is a company's commitment to the health and safety of its employees. We are committed to ensuring and providing a safe environment for all employees at our workplace.

chapters.title: 5.0 Confidentiality
chapters.bodyHtml:
By nature, we deal with confidential and sensitive information about our operations and our customers. Everyone on the team is expected to keep these secure within the company.

It is required that all information about AMIOSH, its customers, and other employees be kept strictly confidential. The release of confidential information, whether intentional or unintentional, can injure AMIOSH and those individuals associated with it. If a team member violates this policy, disciplinary action will be taken, up to and including immediate discharge.

All records and files of AMIOSH are property of AMIOSH and considered confidential. No employee is authorized to copy or disclose any file or record. Confidential information includes all letters or any other information concerning transactions with clients, client lists, payroll or personnel records of past or present employees, financial records of the AMIOSH, all records pertaining to purchases from vendors or suppliers, correspondence and agreements with manufacturers or distributors, and documents concerning operating procedures of AMIOSH.

All telephone calls, letters, or other requests for information about current or former employees should be immediately directed to the management team.

If a team member is unsure if something is confidential or has any questions regarding their responsibilities in dealing with or releasing confidential materials, please ask the management team.

chapters.title: 6.0 Professional Ethics
chapters.bodyHtml:
Team members, by the nature of their work, are in close contact with clients and their staff and bear a major responsibility for strict adherence to the Code of Professional Conduct.

Every member of the professional staff is expected to be thoroughly familiar with the rules of professional conduct.

chapters.title: 7.0 Conduct at the Client's Office/Site
chapters.bodyHtml:
This policy places a special burden on the accountant to maintain a professional demeanor at all times. Rather than leave this important aspect of behavior to personal interpretation, we believe it is necessary to be specific about what we consider to be appropriate conduct.

  1. After spending time with client’s personnel, a close personal relationship may arise. Although a friendly attitude is encouraged, long personal discussions with client employees during working hours should be avoided. Such disruptions of work are likely to be resented by the client’s executives.
  2. As a general rule, discuss with client employees only those matters that are pertinent to their department and level of responsibility.
  3. Do not discuss internal AMIOSH affairs with client employees.
  4. Refrain from discussing personal matters or personal problems with client employees. This specifically includes the subject of your own compensation.
  5. Do not comment on or criticize the work, fees, or other characteristics of other consultants. Support our company, but do not disparage others.
  6. Do not discuss the shortcomings or idiosyncrasies of client personnel.
  7. Avoid all talk pertaining to client matters in elevators, restaurants, public transportation, or other public places.
  8. Do not discuss procedural problems, technical questions, and controversies in the presence of client employees.
  9. Do not make purchases from clients other than at their normal sales price.

chapters.title: 8.0 Policy Against Harassment
chapters.bodyHtml:
We do not tolerate unlawful harassment of any of our team members, clients, vendors, suppliers, or independent contractors. Any form of harassment which violates federal, state, or local law, including but not limited to harassment related to an individual’s race, religion, color, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, uniformed service member status, marital status, pregnancy, age, medical condition (cancer-related or HIV/AIDS-related), or disability is a violation of this policy and will be treated as a disciplinary matter.

For these purposes the term “harassment” includes slurs and any other offensive remarks, jokes, or other verbal, graphic, or physical conduct.

chapters.title: 9.0 Common Rules
chapters.bodyHtml:
Office Hours

Default working time is from 8:30 am to 5:30 pm, Monday through Friday.

Dress Code

Please follow the weekly attire schedule to maintain a professional appearance:

  • Monday: Corporate Shirt (Maroon)
  • Tuesday: AMIOSH Green T-Shirt
  • Wednesday: Black T-Shirt
  • Thursday: Casual Wear (Smart & Neat)
  • Friday: AMIOSH Round Neck T-Shirt

Ensure clothing is clean, tidy, and presentable at all times.

Lunch Break

Lunch is designated from 1:00 pm to 2:00 pm. Snacks in the kitchen are for quick energy boosts. They are not meal replacements.

Cooking facilities are available. Please clean up after yourself to keep our shared space tidy.

Last Person Out

If you’re the last to leave, turn off all lights, lock doors and grilles, and switch off electrical equipment for long holidays.

chapters.title: 10.0 I Need Gear!
chapters.bodyHtml:
AMIOSH will provide you with the equipment you need in order to do your job. Typically, this means a computer, mouse, keyboard, and monitor, but special equipment requests are welcomed and will be approved on a case-by-case basis.

Some things we are happy to provide for you:

  • Laptop/PC
  • Keyboard/mouse
  • Company attire (we do company clothing purchases occasionally)

chapters.title: 11.0 Team Outings
chapters.bodyHtml:
We try our best to plan as many activities as possible. It’s important for us to get to know each other as a team and as people, inside and outside the office. These are work events and all employees are expected to make a conscious effort to attend.

Even outside the office, all employees are expected to behave appropriately towards one another. Do not make anyone feel uncomfortable. Be a good human.

chapters.title: 12.0 Leave Entitlement
chapters.bodyHtml:
12.1 Annual Leave (AL)

a. Please apply at least three days before you'd like to take leave via work.amiosh.com.

b. Annual leave is allocated to all employees as follows:

CategoryEntitlement
Probation / PermanentA – 8 days per year (or pro-rated)
B – 12 days per year (or pro-rated)
C – 16 days per year (or pro-rated)
Additional one-day leave every 3 years of service
Contract & TemporaryAccording to Letter of Appointment (LOA)
InternOne day per month (includes medical leave). Exceeding quota: verbal warning, then deduction.

d. Previous year leave balance can be carried forward to the next year; however, it must be fully utilized by December or credited into frozen leave.

12.2 Emergency Leave (EL)

a. Terms for EL:

  1. Vehicle breakdown during work travel
  2. Natural disaster
  3. Illness of dependent family members (children, spouse, parents)
  4. Accident

b. Process:

  1. Notify management as soon as possible via appropriate communication.
  2. Complete the formal application through work.amiosh.com upon return.
12.3 Medical Leave (MC) & Hospitalization Leave

a. MC entitlement:

  1. 14 days for < 2 years of service
  2. 18 days between 2–5 years of service
  3. 22 days for ≥ 5 years of service

b. Every MC must include an official medical certificate; absence without one is treated as annual leave.

c. Hospitalization leave: 60 days total, with paid sick leave regardless of service period.

d. All MC or hospitalization records must be uploaded to work.amiosh.com.

e. COVID-19 matters follow Ministry of Health (MOH) guidelines.

12.4 Compassionate Leave

a. One (1) day for death of immediate family (grandparents, parents, siblings, spouse, children).

b. Notify management ASAP via appropriate communication.

12.5 Time-Off / Time-Slip

a. Time-off by healthcare provider for self/dependents (max 4 hrs) is not recorded but requires original time-slip; longer durations require leave application.

b. Notify management ASAP via appropriate communication.

12.6 Maternity & Paternity Leave

a. Per Employment Act 2022:

  1. Maternity: 98 days (≥ 90 days service)
  2. Paternity: 7 days (≥ 1 year service)

b. Notify expected due date (EDD) to management via email.

chapters.title: 13.0 Company Expenses
chapters.bodyHtml:
Team members are allowed to be reimbursed for applicable expenses. Here are some examples of what AMIOSH reimburses:

Things AllowedNot Allowed
  1. Mileage (RM 0.60 per km for car & RM 0.30 per km for motorcycle, except distance to & fro >50 km)
  2. Meal with clients (upon management approval)
  3. Professional/software licenses (upon management approval)
  4. Office equipment (upon management approval)
  5. Others (pantry’s food, etc.)
  1. Earphones, headphones, or any gadgets not directly related to scope of job works.
  2. Snacks for consumption during work related travelling. This has been compensated in the offsite work allowance.
Travel Vehicle Priority

*Mileage is applicable only when a company’s car is not available and management approves usage of their own car. The approval shall be made via an appropriate communication method.

chapters.title: 14.0 Getting Paid
chapters.bodyHtml:
When

Team members are paid once a month, on the 26th. Claim forms must be submitted by the 25th. If these dates fall on a weekend or public holiday, payment is made the preceding working day.

How

Salaries are paid via direct deposit, preferably into a CIMB bank account.

Bonuses

Performance‐based bonuses are discretionary, depending on the company’s annual financial results. Typically awarded before Hari Raya and at year‐end.

chapters.title: 15.0 Bonus & Performance Review
chapters.bodyHtml:
The management will consider bonuses based on the company’s annual financial performance for that year. Bonuses are applicable only to permanent team members and to new hires who have completed one year of service from their permanent date.

Bonuses are normally paid before Raya and at the end of the year in December.

chapters.title: 16.0 Benefits
chapters.bodyHtml:
16.1 Public Holidays

At AMIOSH, all employees are paid (and not expected at work) for all public holidays gazetted by the Malaysian federal government and Selangor.

16.2 Medical Card

After the probationary period ends, the company will allocate a medical card according to the company’s insurance provider. If an individual already has their own medical card, the company will reimburse up to the allocation on a receipt basis. The company’s allocation is capped at RM 105/month; any unused balance cannot be claimed.

Note: If the employee resigns after the company’s contribution in the current year, they must reimburse the company according to the notice month.

16.3 Outpatient Claim

All employees are entitled to outpatient claims, capped at RM 300/year. Kindly provide the proofs to HR.

16.4 Yearly Increment

We offer a yearly salary increment of at least RM 100; this is subject to the company’s financial performance for that year.

16.5 Traveling and Accommodation

All travel (flight, company car, etc.) and hotel arrangements will be managed by the office team.

16.6 10-Years Working Service

As an act of appreciation for ten years of service at AMIOSH, we offer a benefit of RM 5,000 to RM 8,000 (depending on the company’s financial performance) for investment purposes.

chapters.title: 17.0 Allowances
chapters.bodyHtml:
17.1 Outstation – Travelling/Overnight Allowance

Employees on outstation duties (overnight or early travel) may claim allowances as below:

CategoryEntitlementTerm
InternRM 30 per night/dayTravel distance ≥ 200 km
ARM 50 per night/day
BRM 80 per night/day
CRM 100 per night/day

T&C:

  • Unawarded projects require management approval.
  • Waived if meals are provided by the company.
17.2 Training/Audit/Consultation on Weekends

Employees conducting training, audits, or consultations on weekends (not covered in 17.3) are entitled as follows:

CategoryEntitlement
ARM 100 per day
BRM 300 per day
CRM 500 per day
17.3 Working on Weekends & Public Holidays

Employees required to work weekends and public holidays (excluding 17.2) are entitled as follows:

CategoryWeekend RatePublic Holiday Rate
ARM 80 per dayRM 130 per day
BRM 100 per dayRM 160 per day
CRM 180 per dayRM 230 per day
17.4 Phone Allowance

Permanent team members receive a phone allowance of RM 60 per month.

17.5 Maternity & Paternity Allowance

A one-off allowance of RM 500 for permanent team members upon maternity or paternity.

17.6 Staff’s Right

Employees may refuse overtime work for valid reasons.

chapters.title: 18.0 Management’s Right
chapters.bodyHtml: The management reserves the right to change these terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. In the event that any changes are made, the revised terms and conditions shall be updated and circulated immediately through appropriate communication means.
